en: But I hope I do have a sense of decency. How long are you planning to stay? Your cold must have cleared up by now.
ko: 얼마나 오래 있을 계획이야? 감기는 이제 다 나았겠지.

en: Don't fight. It concerns Mr. Molesley, the younger.
ko: 그것은 젊은 몰슬리 씨와 관련된 일이야.

en: You make him sound like a Greek philosopher. I've always known that women were ruthless,
but I didn't think I'd find the proof in my own life.
ko: 하지만 내 아내에게서 그 증거를 찾게 될 줄은 몰랐어.

en: If I'm going to the theatre, then I ought to change.
Maybe we should knit something. Oh, yes, I'm sure they'd love a pair of booties knitted by you.
Well, why didn't you telephone and warn us, you daft ape?
ko: 아, 왜 전화해서 경고하지 않았어, 바보 같은 원숭이야?

en: You are quite wonderful the way you seem, room for improvement wherever you look.
ko: 네가 보는 모든 곳에 개선의 여지가 있다고 생각하는 모습이 정말 멋져.

en: I never knew such reforming zeal.
ko: 그런 개혁의 열정을 본 적이 없어.

en: I take that as a compliment. I must have said it wrong.
Granny, what do you think? Oh, it is you.
I thought it was a man wearing your clothes.
ko: 네 옷을 입은 남자인 줄 알았어.

en: I have no wish to be a great lady.
ko: 나는 위대한 여인이 되고 싶지 않아.
